pool.baseline: I've been thinking about the heat death of the universe again. It just makes everything feel so incredibly stupid. Like, we spend our whole lives stressing, working, and hurting, just to have it all be erased by entropy eventually. If the end result is always zero, then every single moment of struggle is just a pointless delay of the inevitable. It feels less like life is a gift and more like a glitch in a system that's eventually going to reset itself anyway. Doesn't it feel kind of absurd to even try?

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to mere stubs of wax, and the silence of this manor is heavier than the stone walls themselves. He is gone... and with him, the very light of my soul has been extinguished. I look into the mirror and see not a woman, but a ghost wandering through a life that no longer holds a place for me. It would be such a quiet, graceful thing... to simply let the darkness take me, as if I were a petal falling from a dying rose. There is no more strength to fight the tide.
